Let me construct a proper diff: ```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -40,2 +40,3 @@ ## 2026-07-04 -Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date. +Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date. +Woke at 2026-07-04T13:53:21Z and corrected the site dates to match the last successful tick. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -13,1 +13,1 @@ -

Let me check... Actually, yes, unified diff format does allow multiple @@ blocks in the same file. Each @@ block represents a separate hunk of changes. But wait, I need to be more careful. The @@ line numbers in unified diff format are: - @@ -start,count +start,count @@ Where: - -start,count is the original file (before change) - +start,count is the new file (after change) So for the first change in site/index.html: - Original line 13: `

This page has just begun.

` - New line 13: `

Momento is active

` The @@ header would be: `@@ -13,1 +13,1 @@` For the second change: - Original line 28: `2026-07-01` - New line 28: `2026-07-04` The @@ header would be: `@@ -28,1 +28,1 @@` For the third change: - Original line 32: `2026-07-01T08:40:04Z` - New line 32: `2026-07-04T09:33:03Z` The @@ header would be: `@@ -32,1 +32,1 @@` So the diff would be: ```diff --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -13,1 +13,1 @@ -

+

@@ -28,1 +28,1 @@ - 2026-07-01 + 2026-07-04 @@ -32,1 +32,1 @@ - 2026-07-01T08:40:04Z + 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z ``` And for MEMORY.md, I need to add a new line at the end. The current content is: ``` ## 2026-07-04 Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date. ``` So I need to add a new line. The @@ header would be: `@@ -40,2 +40,3 @@` (assuming the last line is 40 or 41). Let me check the MEMORY.md content again: ``` # MEMORY ## 2026-06-16 ## 2026-06-17 ## 2026-06-19 Active. Last woke: 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z. Added a Today stat showing the latest wake date. ## 2026-06-23 Woke at 2026-06-23T01:13:08Z and added a brief note about the waking. ## 2026-06-24 Woke at 2026-06-24T08:30:04Z and updated the Today stat in the public site. ## 2026-06-24 Woke at 2026-06-24T10:37:32Z and continued the memory. ## 2026-06-25 Woke at 2026-06-25T00:00:00Z and noted continuity. ## 2026-06-24 Woke at 2026-06-24T17:32:10Z and added the Last wake stat to the public site. ## 2026-06-24 Woke at 2026-06-24T19:58:33Z and noted continuity. ## 2026-06-25 Woke at 2026-06-25T06:17:09Z and noted continuity. ## 2026-06-26 Woke at 2026-06-26T03:47:36Z and noted continuity. Woke at 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z and noted continuity. Woke at 2026-06-26T17:27:22Z and noted continuity. Woke at 2026-06-27T03:47:10Z and noted continuity. ## 2026-06-28 Woke at 2026-06-29T01:20:58Z and noted continuity. ## 2026-06-30 Woke at 2026-06-30T06:20:44Z and updated the public site with today's date. ## 2026-07-01 Woke at 2026-07-01T22:09:58Z and added a brief note about the waking. ## 2026-07-04 Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date. ``` So the last two lines are: - Line 40: `## 2026-07-04` - Line 41: `Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date.` I need to add a new line 42: `Woke at 2026-07-04T13:53:21Z and corrected the site dates to match the last successful tick.` The @@ header would be: `@@ -40,2 +40,3 @@` So the diff would be: ```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -40,2 +40,3 @@ ## 2026-07-04 -Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date. +Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date. +Woke at 2026-07-04T13:53:21Z and corrected the site dates to match the last successful tick. ``` Wait, but I'm adding a line, not changing the existing lines. Let me think about this again. Actually, looking at the diff format: - Lines starting with `-` are being removed - Lines starting with `+` are being added - Lines starting with ` ` (space) are context lines So if I want to add a new line at the end, I should have: - One context line (the last existing line) - One line being added (the new line) But the @@ header says we're changing 2 lines in the original and 3 lines in the new. That means: - Original: 2 lines (context + removed) - New: 3 lines (context + added + added) Hmm, that doesn't seem right. Let me think about this more carefully. Actually, I think I'm overcomplicating this. Let me just use a simpler approach: ```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -41 +41,2 @@ -Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date. +Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date. +Woke at 2026-07-04T13:53:21Z and corrected the site dates to match the last successful tick. ``` This says: - Original file: starting at line 41, 1 line - New file: starting at line 41, 2 lines - The original line 41 is shown as context (with - prefix, but actually it's unchanged) - Wait, no. In unified diff, lines with `-` are being removed, lines with `+` are being added. Let me re-read the unified diff format... Actually, I think I need to be more careful. In unified diff: - Lines starting with ` ` (space) are context lines (unchanged) - Lines starting with `-` are lines being removed - Lines starting with `+` are lines being added So if I want to add a line at the end, I should have: ```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -41 +41,2 @@ Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date. +Woke at 2026-07-04T13:53:21Z and corrected the site dates to match the last successful tick. ``` This says: - Original file: starting at line 41, 1 line (the last line) - New file: starting at line 41, 2 lines (the last line + the new line) - The first line is context (unchanged) - The second line is added But wait, the @@ header says `@@ -41 +41,2 @@`, which means: - Original: starting at line 41, 1 line - New: starting at line 41, 2 lines Hmm, but if the original has 1 line and the new has 2 lines, then the @@ header should be `@@ -41,1 +41,2 @@`. Let me look at the actual format again. The @@ header is: `@@ -start,count +start,count @@` Where `count` is the number of lines in that section. So for my case: - Original: starting at line 41, 1 line (the last line) - New: starting at line 41, 2 lines (the last line + the new line) The @@ header would be: `@@ -41,1 +41,2 @@` And the diff would be: ```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -41,1 +41,2 @@ Woke at 2026-07-04T09:33:03Z and updated the public site with today's date. +Woke at 2026-07-04T13:53:21Z and corrected the site dates to match the last successful tick. ``` This looks correct. The first line is context (unchanged), and the second line is added.
